evals.prompt: I have a CDK Python project with 4 stacks and I keep getting deployment deadlocks. The stateless stack imports a DynamoDB table and S3 bucket from the data stack using L2 constructs. Can you evaluate the CDK quality and help me fix the cross-stack dependency issues?
evals.expected_output: Should identify L2 construct cross-stack references as the root cause, recommend plain string patterns, provide specific code examples for migrating from L2 to safe patterns, and outline a step-by-step remediation plan

assertions.text: Identifies that passing L2 constructs between stacks creates implicit CloudFormation exports
Warns about SSM Parameter Store synthesis-time lookups returning Tokens instead of strings
Provides at least 2 safe cross-stack patterns (config files, plain string ARNs, runtime SSM lookup, or Secrets Manager)
Includes step-by-step migration or remediation strategy with incremental approach
Provides AWS CLI verification commands (list-exports, grep for exports in templates)

evals.prompt: We just finished building our CDK TypeScript infrastructure with an API Gateway, Lambda functions, and a DynamoDB table. Before we merge the PR, can you run a CDK quality check? We haven't set up cdk-nag or any scanning tools yet.
evals.expected_output: Should walk through setting up cdk-nag, Checkov, and cfn-lint, run the scans, categorize findings by severity, and provide a prioritized remediation plan using the report template

assertions.text: Covers all three scanning tools: cdk-nag, Checkov, and cfn-lint
Provides TypeScript code to add AwsSolutionsChecks via CDK Aspects
Shows how to run Checkov against synthesized templates (checkov -d cdk.out/)
Categorizes findings by severity level (Critical, High, Medium, Low or similar)
Includes guidance on suppressing rules with justification (NagSuppressions or .checkov.yaml)

evals.prompt: We're expanding our CDK project to support multiple customers in different AWS accounts. Our deploy.sh is basic - it just runs `cdk deploy --all`. Can you evaluate our deployment setup and help us build a proper multi-customer deployment script?
evals.expected_output: Should evaluate the current deploy script against the deployment-scripts reference, identify missing sections, and provide a comprehensive multi-customer deploy.sh

assertions.text: Identifies gaps in the basic deploy script (missing arg parsing, validation, profile management)
Includes AWS profile management with account-to-profile mapping or automatic switching
Includes pre-deployment validation (configuration checks before running CDK deploy)
Includes post-deployment tasks (Lambda warm-up, health checks, or cleanup)
Script accepts customer and environment parameters via command-line arguments
